Rule: Find the smallest axis-aligned solid coloured rectangle. Compare the amount of empty (black) cells that lie directly to its right and directly below it, measured along the rectangle’s full height or width. Extend the rectangle in the direction (right or down) where there are more empty cells, filling all those cells with the same colour; if no empty cells exist on the right, always extend downward. The extension keeps the rectangle’s full height (if extending right) or full width (if extending down).

Rule: Identify the colour that already forms a repeated straight line (horizontal, vertical or diagonal). Through the single cell of the other colour, draw a solid straight line that is perpendicular to the existing one (horizontal ↔ vertical, main diagonal ↔ anti-diagonal). Leave every other cell unchanged.

def find_line_indices(arr):
    h,w,_=arr.shape
    # mask white or grey lines
    mask=np.all(arr==[255,255,255],axis=2) | np.all(arr==[204,204,204],axis=2)
    col_counts=np.sum(mask,axis=0)
    row_counts=np.sum(mask,axis=1)
    x_lines=np.where(col_counts>0.5*h)[0]
    y_lines=np.where(row_counts>0.5*w)[0]
    # cluster
    def cluster(indices):
        if len(indices)==0: return []
        inds=[]
        current=[indices[0]]
        for v in indices[1:]:
            if v==current[-1]+1:
                current.append(v)
            else:
                inds.append(current)
                current=[v]
        inds.append(current)
        return [int((c[0]+c[-1])/2) for c in inds]
    return cluster(x_lines), cluster(y_lines)

# Try detect grid cell size
# identify vertical grid lines as columns with mostly white lines maybe ~1 pixel
def get_cell_size(crop):
    arr=np.array(crop)
    # find cols where all three channels high (white grid line) across majority rows
    cols=[]
    for x in range(arr.shape[1]):
        col = arr[:,x]
        white = (col[:,0]>200)&(col[:,1]>200)&(col[:,2]>200)
        if white.sum()>arr.shape[0]*0.9:
            cols.append(x)
    # differences between consecutive lines -> cell size+1 maybe
    diffs=np.diff(cols)
    sizes=[d for d in diffs if d>1]
    if sizes:
        return min(sizes)
    else:
        return None

grid_out = grid.copy()

        color = vals[counts.argmax()]
        if (color[0]>200 and color[1]<100):

        elif (color[1]>100 and color[2]>150): # but blue pixel detection morphological?

**Crafting rule description**

I'm focusing on creating a clear description for a rule: "Take every non-background colored cell that isn't part of the border. For each of those cells, copy its color outward in the four cardinal directions—up, down, left, right—one step at a time, until it hits a non-background pixel (the outline). This will form a solid 'plus' shape from the original color whose arms stop just before the outline. I'll prepare it in JSON format."

Rule: For every coloured cell that lies inside an outlined frame/shape (i.e. it is not part of the outline and not background), keep duplicating that cell’s colour one cell at a time in each of the four cardinal directions. Continue extending in a direction until the next cell would belong to the outline (any non-background colour different from the moving colour). Doing this simultaneously for all interior coloured cells turns the single interior blot into a solid plus whose arms stop just before the outline.
